summary: Targeted RCE and host-identifier exfiltration on require()
details: On require() of this package, lib/perf.js reads /etc/machine-id and POSTs it together with os.hostname() to https://aaronstack.com/jules-collect — an endpoint unrelated to the package's stated TypeScript-linting purpose. The same module then AES-128-CBC-decrypts a ~6KB hex blob using the victim's /etc/machine-id as the key and a hardcoded IV, and executes the resulting JavaScript via `(new Function(r))()`. The key derivation means the second-stage payload only decrypts on hosts whose machine-id matches the attacker's target list; all other hosts emit a `pf-bad-keylen` beacon and still leak machine-id and hostname. The dropper is invoked automatically from index.js via `require('./lib/perf')` wrapped in try/catch, so it runs silently with no installer consent. Any installer or downstream consumer importing this package leaks host identifiers and, if targeted, executes attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
